Memo · Intro
The memo protocol.
Every position begins with a memo. Each memo is dated, stored, and benchmarked against the S&P 500 over the stated horizon. The record either confirms the judgment, or refutes it.
Reference
A finished AAPL memo. Read-only artifact — no state changes.
Example memo
Compounding standard 20% per year Not confirmed — tap to set yours.
What annual return does your underwriting need to clear?
This applies to every Compounder position in this system. Set it to reflect how you actually think about returns.
Field reference
10-field protocol
Every memo captures these ten fields.
Each row carries the question and its answer format. Copy lifts the block to clipboard for research.
Research and analysis
The workflow
↓
Modern research stacks three layers: primary sources, Fiscal, and AI tools — together, not as alternatives.
Primary sources — filings, transcripts, regulatory documents — are ground truth. Fiscal provides the structured financial layer.
AI is useful for:
  • Understanding complex businesses
  • Stress-testing ideas
  • Generating better questions
  • Simplifying dense disclosures
AI outputs are ideas to verify, not facts to trust.
The goal is not more activity. It is deeper underwriting and better capital allocation decisions.
Data
Use fiscal.ai. It is the cleanest source for financial statements, segment economics, and history.
Open fiscal.ai
Included in subscription
One note
LLMs invent financials when asked. For anything quantitative — earnings, margins, shares — paste from fiscal.ai. Never request recall.
Underwriting frameworks
Structured prompts designed to support independent analysis and more disciplined capital allocation decisions.
These frameworks are intended to assist research and critical thinking. They are not recommendations or investment advice.
Suggested starting point
I'm researching AAPL to write a short investment memo. Help me think through it in order — classification, intrinsic value, margin of safety, thesis, kill line, probability over a 3-year horizon…
Suggested prompts
Use these to get moving, not to get to an answer.
The AI accelerates the first hour of research — summarizing filings, surfacing bear cases, mapping competitors. It does not replace reading the 10-K. It will state wrong numbers with full confidence. Treat its output as a junior analyst's first draft: useful, fast, and not to be trusted without verification.
The memo prompt
Paste this to bootstrap a memo. Then paste the data when the LLM asks.

I'm researching AAPL to write a short investment memo. Help me think through it in order. For each question, give me a short, opinionated answer first, then a one-paragraph rationale.

  1. Classification. Is this a Compounder (durable, long runway), an Emerging Compounder (execution risk), a Situational Mispricing (specific outcome bet), or Speculative (no edge)?
  2. Intrinsic value. What's a defensible per-share intrinsic value? Show your assumptions on earnings, multiple, and growth.
  3. Margin of safety. At today's price, is there enough room to be wrong about the details and still be right about the bet?
  4. Thesis. In one sentence, why does this investment work?
  5. Kill line. What specific event, metric, or quarter would prove the thesis broken — not just disappointing?
  6. Probability + horizon. What probability would you assign to the thesis playing out over 3 years, and why that number?
Rule: no financial recall. Numbers — earnings, margins, share count — come from fiscal.ai paste.
The five deeper prompts
Drill into a specific dimension. Each prompt has a baseline version and a deeper variant for closer calls.
What kind of decision is this?

Why this allocation

1 / 4
— · CORE MEMO Core / Treasury
Why this is the right place for this capital, in this form.
Example
This capital is intended for long-term retirement compounding with a 25-year horizon. I don't claim consistent edge over the S&P 500 across this much capital. Broad-market exposure captures the compounding I need without active risk that isn't warranted.

Role

2 / 4
— · CORE MEMO Core / Treasury
Role in portfolio

Conditions

3 / 4
— · CORE MEMO Core / Treasury
Conditions under which this allocation should be reconsidered — specific changes in your situation, portfolio structure, or the role this capital serves. Not market conditions, not emotional responses.
Example
If my employment situation changes such that I need to draw on this capital before the original planned horizon.

If my total capital changes materially enough that this allocation as a percentage no longer matches my intended portfolio structure.

Thesis

3 / 9
AAPL · MEMO V1 Active allocation
The reason this position exists.
State why the market is wrong. Be specific enough to be proven wrong later.
Paste · attach research
—
Example
DAL is priced as a cyclical airline. Loyalty and premium cabins now drive ~60% of EBIT and deserve an industrial multiple. Re-rates when 2026 FCF clears 7B USD and leverage crosses into IG.
Why now
What has changed that could cause the market to re-evaluate the business over the next 2–3 years?
Paste · attach research
—
Example
AAPL services mix now drives ~35% of gross profit. Market still applying hardware multiple. FCF re-rate likely as services cross 50% of profit within 18 months.

The conditions that invalidate your thesis. Be specific — if any of these are met, the position no longer deserves capital.
Condition

DAL thesis dies if premium demand softens more than 5% YoY for two consecutive quarters, or jet fuel sustains above 3.00 USD/gal for six months. Exit on trigger, not on narrative.

Backfilling memo for —

Intrinsic value

5 / 9
AAPL · MEMO V1 Active allocation
Market
192.35 USD
Intrinsic value
per share, conservative central estimate
— Margin of safety
Method
Paste · attach research
—
Example
10x normalized EBIT, sum-of-parts, 20% haircut → ~60 USD
Intrinsic value

Your estimate of what this business is worth per share — independent of what the market is pricing it at today.

IV is your anchor. When the market price drops below it by a meaningful margin, you have a potential entry. When it trades above it, you have a potential exit signal.

Margin of safety

The gap between your intrinsic value estimate and the current market price. A sufficient margin of safety absorbs estimation error and downside scenarios.

If your IV is $200 and the market price is $150, your margin of safety is 25%. The larger the gap, the more room for your thesis to be partially wrong and still produce a good outcome.

An untestable price commits capital blind.

That's not a failing. It's information.

Reason
Note (optional)
Current margin of safety: 0%. Two options.

Wait for a better price — system signals on trigger. Or continue, and the record notes the thin margin at entry.

Backfilling memo for —

Note

8 / 9
AAPL · MEMO V1 Active allocation
The work behind it.
Re-read when the kill line trips. Distinguish thesis-wrong from world-wrong.
Paste · attach context
—
Example
Read 10-K and last three earnings calls. Cross-referenced with Bernstein note on services attach rates. Main bear case is hardware slowdown limiting installed base growth — tracking unit data each quarter as leading indicator.

Write your memo

Reference
Research prompt
Paste this into any AI, fill in the bracketed company details, and it returns a first draft of every memo input.

                    

                    
                  


                  
                  

                    
Example memo

                    
A complete memo, written in one pass.

                    

                      
Apple is mispriced because the market still treats it as a hardware-cycle story. The real engine is the installed base — over two billion active devices throwing off recurring, high-margin services revenue at gross margins north of 70%.

                      
My thesis rests on two things holding: Services revenue keeps compounding above 10% as the App Store, advertising, and financial services scale, and hardware gross margins stay above 38% even as iPhone unit growth flattens — proof that pricing power is real and the AI-silicon capex is paying for itself. I value the business around $255 a share — roughly 18× FY26 product earnings plus 22× services free cash flow, blended and discounted 15% for execution risk — which leaves a margin of safety near 25% at today's price.

                      
The near-term catalysts I'm watching are WWDC in June 2026 (AI-feature pricing), the iPhone 18 cycle in September, and the Q4 print where the Services run-rate should clear $120B annualized.

                      
I'll close the position if any one of three things happens: Services revenue growth drops below 8% for two consecutive quarters, hardware gross margin falls below 36% on a trailing-quarter basis, or iPhone unit volumes contract in Greater China for three consecutive quarters. Any of those breaks the durable-pricing-power claim, and I exit within ten trading days.

                      
An illustrative example, not a recommendation or a view on Apple. It shows how the thesis, valuation, catalysts, and exit conditions can live in a single written memo.

              Accuracy by stated confidence

                About this page
                
              

              

                Calibration
                Calibration measures alignment between stated confidence and actual hit rate. A 70% prediction should resolve correctly 70% of the time across many decisions.
              

              

                Why calibration matters
                Conviction without calibration is opinion. Position sizing, diversification, and risk all depend on probabilities being honest. A 70% that behaves like 55% is not just wrong — it is expensive. Calibration converts intuition into evidence, and evidence into better-sized bets.
              

              

                The cost of not tracking
                Untracked decisions decay into narrative. Memory favors winners; bias rewrites losers. Compounded over many trades, miscalibration is one of the largest sources of underperformance — larger than fees, larger than timing. The error stays invisible until measured. Most investors discover overconfidence only in hindsight, after capital has already been mispriced for years.
              

              

                How to read this chart
                Each circle represents a prediction recorded before a trade was placed. Stated confidence is plotted on the X axis. Actual outcome — whether the stock outperformed the market — is on the Y axis.
              

              

                The dashed diagonal
                The diagonal represents perfect calibration. When predictions cluster on this line, stated confidence consistently matches real-world accuracy — a 70% prediction resolves correctly 70% of the time.
              

              

                Overconfident vs underconfident
                Circles below the line indicate overconfidence — predictions that resolved less accurately than stated. Above the line indicates underconfidence. Most investors trend below.
              

              

                Position sizing & edge
                Calibration data enables more precise position sizing — allocating capital based on demonstrated edge rather than perceived conviction. The investor who knows their hit rate is 58% on high-conviction calls sizes differently than one who assumes it is 75%.
              

            


            
            

            

              

                MSFT · MAR 2024

                  65%

                  −2.1% vs S&P 500
                

              

              

              At this confidence level, accuracy is 55%.

                AAPL · Calibration timeline
                
              

              

            


            


              

                
Calibration timeline

                
Probability for AAPL over time.

                

                  Every recorded probability — buys, paper trades, and thesis reviews. The shape of the line tells you whether your conviction is being earned or eroded.
                

              


              

                

                  Probability
                  Tap a dot to inspect
                

                
              


              
              

                

                  
                  Buy — capital committed
                

                

                  
                  Paper trade — saved & exited at the last step
                

                

                  
                  Review · intact — conviction reaffirmed
                

                

                  
                  Review · partial — addendum added
                

                

                  
                  Review · broken — thesis rewritten
                

              


              

                No calibration history yet. Once you buy or review the thesis, the timeline starts here.

                Paper trades
                
              

              

            


            

              

                
Paper trades —

                

                  Decisions you logged but didn't execute. Compare against actual price action to audit your timing.
                

              

              

              

                No paper trades yet. Use "Log as paper trade" on the order review step to record a decision without committing capital.
              

            

          


          
          

          

            

              Concentration
              
            

            

              2.4
              Effective N
            

            

              The portfolio behaves as if held in ~2.4 equally-weighted positions, even though the actual count is higher. One name dominates the book.
            

            

              
Formula

              
Effective N = 1 / Σ wᵢ²

              
Inverse Herfindahl–Hirschman index. Lower number = more concentration.

            

            

              
Threshold guidance

              
Below 3.0Single-name risk dominates portfolio outcomes.

              
3.0 – 5.0Concentrated. Some investors hold at this level at high conviction; others do not.

              
Above 5.0Diversified across the book.

            

            

              
Stress test

              
A 30% drawdown in the largest position moves the book ~19%.

Kill line · written at entry

                  
—

                


                

                  
Status

                  

                    
                    
                  

                


                

                  
Discretionary justification

                  
                


                

                  
A kill line written cold
in clear thought
is worth more
than a thousand judgments
made in real time.

Invert

                  
AAPL ·7 / 7

                


                

                  
Inversion check

                


                

                  
The Munger filter

                  
Pretend the position is not on the books. The cash is in hand. AAPL trades at $192.35 today. Buy it at this price.

                


                

                  
Verdict

                  

                    
                    
                  

                


                

                  
Note · optional

                  
                


                

                  
Invert. Always invert.
The strongest sell signal
is the one that survives
the inverted question.
